name: Settings.inject_litellm_env
docstring:
Export provider API keys into the process environment so that LiteLLM
can pick them up automatically.  Call once at application startup.

name: write_env
docstring: Upsert KEY=value in an .env file.

name: confirm
docstring:
Ask the user to confirm.  If require_yes=True the user must type
the exact word 'yes' (not just 'y') to proceed.

name: build_agent
docstring: Return the raw ADK Agent — used by the local gateway Runner.

name: build_adk_app
docstring: Return an AdkApp wrapping the full agent graph — used for Agent Runtime deploy only.

name: _resolve_env_vars
docstring: Substitute ${VAR:-default} patterns using os.environ.

name: load_agents_yaml
docstring: Parse agents.yaml and return the list of agent config dicts.

name: build_agents_from_yaml
docstring:
Load agents.yaml and build all configured sub-agents.

Returns a list of LlmAgent instances in the order they appear in the YAML.

name: _build_generic
docstring: Build a generic LlmAgent from a YAML config dict.

name: finish_task
docstring:
Signal that the long-running task is fully complete.

Call this when you have produced the final result and there is nothing
more to do.  Pass a concise summary of the outcome as `summary`.

Args:
    summary: Human-readable summary of what was accomplished.

Returns:
    A completion marker dict (do not parse; just return it to the user).

name: build_task_agent
docstring:
Build the long-running task LoopAgent.

Returns:
    A LoopAgent that autonomously executes multi-step tasks, iterating
    up to MAX_ITERATIONS times before stopping.

name: run_agent
docstring:
Run the Hermes agent for a connector message and return the full text reply.

Args:
    platform: Short platform tag used for session namespacing, e.g. "telegram".
    platform_user_id: Stable user identifier from the platform.
    message: The user's text message.

Returns:
    The agent's full plain-text response.

name: _verify_slack_signature
docstring: Verify Slack's HMAC-SHA256 request signature.

name: slack_webhook
docstring:
Receive a Slack Events API payload.

Handles:
  - url_verification challenge (required during app setup)
  - message events (DMs and app mentions)

name: _verify_teams_token
docstring: Verify the Bot Framework JWT. Returns True if valid.

name: teams_webhook
docstring:
Receive a Bot Framework Activity from Microsoft Teams and reply.

Handles Activity type 'message'; all other types are acknowledged silently.

name: _send_teams_reply
docstring: Obtain a Bot Framework access token and post a reply activity.

name: _send_message
docstring: Send a text reply to a Telegram chat.

name: telegram_webhook
docstring:
Receive an Update from Telegram and reply via the Bot API.

Telegram sends POST requests with a JSON body containing an Update object.
We only handle message updates (text messages); other update types are ignored.

name: score_response
docstring: Score a response against expected keywords. Fully offline.

name: log_quality_score
signature: log_quality_score(user_id, agent_name, query, response, metrics, config)
docstring: Async — logs a quality score row to BigQuery. Fails silently.

name: build_online_monitor
docstring: Returns a MonitorConfig if GCP_PROJECT_ID is set, else None.

name: AgentGatewayConfig
docstring:
Configuration for Agent Gateway routing.

All fields are optional — unset fields cause graceful fallback to
direct Runner execution.

name: AgentGatewayClient
docstring:
Async client for sending chat requests via Agent Gateway.

When gateway is disabled (config.enabled == False), all methods
return None and the caller falls back to direct Runner execution.

name: AgentGatewayClient.send_message
docstring:
Send a message via Agent Gateway and return the JSON response.

Returns None if the gateway is disabled or the call fails (graceful fallback).

name: AgentGatewayClient.stream_message
docstring:
Stream SSE events from Agent Gateway.

Falls back to empty iterator if gateway is disabled or fails.
The caller (gateway/main.py) detects the empty iterator and uses
the direct ADK runner instead.

name: AgentGatewayClient.close
docstring: Close the underlying HTTP client. Call on app shutdown.

name: build_agent_gateway
docstring:
Build an AgentGatewayClient from settings.

Returns a disabled client (config.enabled=False) when
AGENT_GATEWAY_ENDPOINT is not set, so all calls fall through
to direct Runner execution transparently.

name: verify_google_token
docstring:
Validate a Google ID token and return the decoded claims.

Raises HTTP 401 on invalid/expired token.
Cached for 5 minutes to avoid a Google round-trip on every request.

When DISABLE_AUTH=true (local dev only), skips all validation and returns
a synthetic "local-dev" user so every endpoint works without a token.

name: chat
docstring:
Stream chat responses as Server-Sent Events.

Each SSE event is a JSON-encoded ChatEvent.
Final event has type='done'.

name: _stream_agent
docstring: Yield SSE-formatted JSON strings for each agent event.

name: list_sessions
docstring: List active sessions for the authenticated user.

name: clear_memories
docstring: Clear all long-term memories for the authenticated user.

name: list_memories
docstring: Retrieve long-term memories for the authenticated user.

name: create_memory
docstring: Directly write a memory fact for the authenticated user (memory-as-a-tool).

name: submit_task
docstring:
Submit a long-running task to the ReAct LoopAgent.

Returns immediately with a task_id and status='pending'.
The task runs in the background for up to 1 hour.

Poll GET /tasks/{task_id} for status and results.

name: get_task
docstring:
Poll the status of a long-running task.

status values:
  pending   — queued, not yet started
  running   — LoopAgent is actively working
  done      — completed successfully; result field contains the answer
  failed    — an error occurred; error field contains the message
  cancelled — client called DELETE /tasks/{task_id}

progress is a list of intermediate notes from the agent.

name: cancel_task
docstring: Cancel a running task. No-op if the task is already complete.

name: list_my_tasks
docstring: List all tasks submitted by the authenticated user (most recent first).

name: scheduler_trigger
docstring:
Webhook called by Cloud Scheduler to trigger an agent task.

Authentication: Cloud Scheduler attaches an OIDC token issued for the
Hermes service account.  We verify it matches our expected service account
to prevent unauthorised task injection.

This endpoint intentionally does NOT use the normal Google OAuth2 CurrentUser
dependency — Cloud Scheduler is a server-to-server caller, not a human user.

name: _verify_scheduler_oidc_token
docstring:
Verify a Google OIDC token and confirm it was issued for our service account.
Raises HTTPException 401/403 on failure.

name: setup_tracing
docstring:
Initialise OpenTelemetry with the Google Cloud Trace exporter.

Safe to call at startup — silently degrades when the packages are
absent so local development is unaffected.

name: instrument_fastapi
docstring: Auto-instrument a FastAPI app to emit HTTP request/response spans.

name: get_tracer
docstring: Return the active tracer, or a no-op stub when tracing is disabled.

name: agent_span
docstring:
Context manager that wraps one agent turn in a Cloud Trace span.

Usage:
    with agent_span("HRAgent", user_id=uid, session_id=sid) as span:
        # run agent turn
        span.set_attribute("hermes.message_len", len(message))

Falls back to a no-op context manager when tracing is not configured.

name: _get_firestore_client
docstring: Return a cached synchronous Firestore client.

name: _fs_doc
docstring: Return the Firestore DocumentReference for a task.

name: _persist
docstring: Write/merge the task record to Firestore (non-blocking via thread).

name: load_task_from_firestore
docstring: Load a task record from Firestore (used after an instance restart).

name: _run_task_background
docstring: Background coroutine: runs the LoopAgent for a task.

name: _public_record
docstring: Strip internal-only fields before returning to the client.

name: submit_task
docstring:
Submit a long-running task.  Returns the task record (status=pending).
The background runner is started as a fire-and-forget asyncio.Task.

name: get_task
docstring: Return the public (serialisable) task record, or None if not found.

name: cancel_task
docstring: Cancel a running task. Returns True if a cancellation was requested.

name: list_user_tasks
docstring: Return all task records for a given user (most recent first).

name: PolicyEngine.check_response
docstring: Check an agent response against all applicable policies.

name: PolicyEngine.check_prompt
docstring: Check a user prompt against all applicable policies.

name: build_policy_engine
docstring: Build a PolicyEngine from policies.yaml. Returns None on failure.

name: build_context_summary
docstring:
Build a compact memory summary for system prompt injection.

Args:
    profile:       User profile (Tier 1). If None, skipped.
    skills:        Relevant skills ranked by recency/relevance (Tier 2).
    budget_tokens: Maximum tokens to spend on the entire summary.

Returns:
    A plain-text string ready for system prompt injection.
    Returns an empty string if nothing fits in the budget.

name: prioritise_memory
docstring:
Trim a list of Skill objects to fit within *budget* tokens.

Items earlier in the list have higher priority (pass pre-ranked list).
Returns the subset that fits.

name: _query_corpus
docstring: Query a single corpus. Returns empty list on failure.

name: retrieve_cross_corpus
docstring:
Query multiple corpora, merge results, sort by score, and deduplicate.

Args:
    query: The search query string.
    corpora: List of corpus resource names or RagCorpus objects.
    top_k: Maximum number of results to return after merging.

Returns:
    A sorted, deduplicated list of RetrievedContext (up to top_k).

name: HermesMemoryBank
docstring:
Application-level facade over VertexAiMemoryBank.

Usage:
    bank = HermesMemoryBank(resource_name="projects/.../memoryBanks/...")
    await bank.generate_memories(user_id="u123", conversation_turn=turn)
    memories = await bank.fetch_memories(user_id="u123", query="VPN setup")

name: HermesMemoryBank.generate_memories
docstring:
Distil a conversation turn into durable memories.

Called from skill_learning_callback (fire-and-forget) after every agent turn.
The SDK call is blocking — we wrap it in asyncio.to_thread.

Args:
    user_id:    The authenticated user identifier.
    user_text:  The user's message text.
    agent_text: The agent's response text.
    agent_name: Optional agent name for metadata.

name: HermesMemoryBank.ingest_events
docstring:
Stream conversation events to Memory Bank for automatic batched memory generation.

More production-grade than generate_memories() — the SDK batches events
and triggers memory generation automatically via IngestEvents RPC.

Args:
    user_id: The authenticated user identifier.
    events:  List of event dicts with keys 'role' ('user'|'agent') and 'text'.

Example:
    await bank.ingest_events(user_id="u1", events=[
        {"role": "user",  "text": "How do I reset my VPN?"},
        {"role": "agent", "text": "Go to Settings > VPN > Reset."},
    ])

name: HermesMemoryBank.purge_memories
docstring:
Bulk-delete all memories for a user.

Uses MemoryBankService.PurgeMemories with a user_id filter.
This is the correct implementation for DELETE /memories/{user_id}.

Args:
    user_id: The user whose memories to delete.
    dry_run: If True, returns count without deleting (default: False).

Returns:
    Number of memories deleted (or that would be deleted on dry_run).

name: HermesMemoryBank.delete_memory
docstring:
Delete a specific memory by its resource name.

Args:
    memory_resource_name: Full resource name, e.g.
        "projects/p/locations/l/memoryBanks/b/memories/m"

Returns:
    True on success, False on failure.

name: HermesMemoryBank.create_memory
docstring:
Directly write a memory fact without LLM extraction/consolidation.

Useful for the 'memory-as-a-tool' pattern where the agent explicitly
decides what to remember, bypassing automatic extraction.

Args:
    user_id: The user this memory belongs to.
    fact:    The plain-text fact to store.

Returns:
    The new memory's resource name, or None on failure.

name: HermesMemoryBank.update_memory
docstring:
Update an existing memory with a corrected fact.

Args:
    memory_resource_name: Full resource name of the memory to update.
    new_fact: The corrected/updated fact text.

Returns:
    True on success, False on failure.

name: HermesMemoryBank.retrieve_profiles
docstring:
Retrieve structured memory profiles for a user.

RetrieveProfiles returns a higher-level view than RetrieveMemories —
facts are organised into a structured profile object per scope.

Returns:
    List of profile dicts with keys 'scope' and 'facts'.

name: HermesMemoryBank.fetch_memories
docstring:
Retrieve the most relevant memories for a user.

Called at session start (PreloadMemoryTool) to inject user context
into the system prompt.

Returns:
    List of memory strings, ready for system prompt injection.

name: HermesMemoryBank.list_revisions
docstring:
Return revision history for all memories belonging to a user.
Useful for debugging and the DELETE /memories/{user_id} endpoint.

name: HermesMemoryBank.format_for_prompt
docstring:
Fetch memories and format them as a system prompt snippet.

Returns an empty string if no memories are found or MemoryBank is unavailable.
The caller (gateway/main.py) injects this into the session system prompt.

name: build_memory_bank
docstring:
Build a HermesMemoryBank from settings.

Returns None if MEMORY_BANK_RESOURCE_NAME is not configured (graceful degradation).

name: create_memory_bank
docstring:
Create a new MemoryBank resource. Returns the resource name.
Safe to call multiple times — returns existing resource if found.

name: _build_extractor_agent
docstring: Build the SkillExtractor agent using the configured lightweight model.

name: extract_skill
docstring:
Run the extractor agent and parse the result.

Returns a Skill if one was extracted, None otherwise.

name: build_skill_learning_callback
docstring:
Return an after_agent_callback coroutine bound to `agent_name`.

ADK signature: async (callback_context) -> None

name: _persist_to_memory_bank
docstring: Write this conversation turn to the native VertexAiMemoryBank.

name: _learn_in_background
docstring: Background task: extract and persist a skill without blocking the response.

name: _extract_text
docstring: Extract plain text from an ADK Content object or string.

name: load_skills_from_dir
docstring:
Scan *skills_dir* for ``*.md`` files and parse each one into a Skill.

Files that fail to parse are skipped with a warning.
``TEMPLATE.md`` is always skipped.

Args:
    skills_dir: Path to the skills directory.  Defaults to ``skills/``
                at the project root.

Returns:
    List of parsed Skill objects (may be empty if no valid files found).

name: _iter_skill_files
docstring: Yield all *.md files under directory, excluding TEMPLATE.md.

name: _parse_skill_file
docstring:
Parse a single skill markdown file.

Returns a Skill on success, None if the file should be silently skipped
(e.g. no frontmatter — likely a README or notes file).

Raises ValueError / KeyError for files that have frontmatter but are
missing required fields, so the caller can log a useful warning.

name: _extract_procedure
docstring: Extract ordered list items from the Steps section of the markdown body.

name: _infer_domain
docstring: Best-effort domain from agent name (e.g. 'AnalyticsAgent' -> 'analytics').

name: Skill
docstring: A single versioned, agent-generated skill.

name: Skill.to_rag_text
docstring: Serialise to text suitable for RAG corpus ingestion.

name: search_skills
docstring:
Retrieve the top-k current skills from the skills corpus matching `query`.
Skips entries that fail to parse (corpus may contain non-skill documents).

name: _parse_rag_text
docstring: Parse a Skill back from its to_rag_text() representation.

name: upsert_skill
docstring:
Insert a new skill or create a new version if a near-duplicate exists.

Near-duplicate detection: search for the skill's trigger text; if a result
with the same skill_id and score >= VERSION_THRESHOLD exists, archive it first.

name: _upload_skill
docstring:
Upload skill as a RagFile text blob.

rag.upload_file is a blocking SDK call — wrapping it in asyncio.to_thread
prevents it from stalling the event loop under high concurrency.

name: UserProfile
docstring: Stable facts about a user, persisted across sessions.

name: UserProfile.to_prompt_summary
docstring: Return a compact string suitable for injection into a system prompt.

name: UserProfile.prompt_token_estimate
docstring: Rough token estimate (4 chars ≈ 1 token).

name: _get_firestore_client
docstring: Lazy import — avoids import errors when Firestore is not available.

name: get_or_create_profile
docstring:
Fetch the user profile from Firestore, or create a minimal one if absent.

Never raises — returns a minimal profile on any error so the caller is
never blocked by a Firestore outage.

name: update_profile
docstring:
Upsert profile fields for a user.

Only fields present in updates are modified; other fields are preserved.
Always stamps last_seen to current UTC time.

name: resolve_model_str
docstring:
Normalise the raw model string.

Returns the model string unchanged (LiteLlm wrapping happens in get_model).

name: get_model
docstring:
Return the correct ADK model value for the given model string.

- Native Gemini IDs are returned as plain strings.
- Everything else is wrapped in google.adk.models.LiteLlm so that
  OpenAI, Anthropic, Bedrock, Ollama, etc. all work transparently.

Args:
    model_str: Model identifier in "<provider>/<model>" format for
               non-Gemini models, or bare Gemini model name.

Returns:
    str | LiteLlm: Value suitable for LlmAgent(model=...).

name: _is_native_gemini
docstring: Return True if model_str refers to a native Gemini/Vertex AI model.

name: HermesAgentRegistry
docstring: Registry backed by Vertex AI agent registry with graceful fallback.

name: HermesAgentRegistry._vertex_register
docstring: Synchronous Vertex AI register call (run in thread).

name: HermesAgentRegistry._vertex_list
docstring: Synchronous Vertex AI list call (run in thread).

name: HermesAgentRegistry.register_agent
docstring: Register an agent. Returns its resource ID.

name: HermesAgentRegistry.list_agents
docstring: Return all registered agents.

name: HermesAgentRegistry.get_agent
docstring: Look up a single agent by name. Returns None if not found.

name: build_registry
docstring: Build a HermesAgentRegistry. Returns None on unrecoverable failure.

name: chat_sse
docstring:
Send a chat message via SSE stream.
Returns (response_text, new_session_id).

name: submit_task
docstring: Submit a long-running task and return the task_id.

name: poll_until_done
docstring: Poll a task until it reaches a terminal state or timeout.

name: test_self_scheduling
docstring: Test the self-scheduling feature via chat.

name: generate_sales_data
docstring: Generate 12 months × 4 regions × 3 products of sales data.

name: upload_doc
docstring: Upload a local text file to a RAG corpus.

name: upload_skill
docstring: Write a skill to a temp file and upload it to the skills corpus.

name: create_corpus
docstring: Create a RAG corpus and return its resource name.

name: _patch_all
docstring: Return a fresh dict of patch objects — call start/stop per test.

name: _FakeLlmAgent
docstring: Lightweight stand-in for google.adk.agents.LlmAgent.

name: _FakeEventSourceResponse
docstring: Minimal stub so FastAPI accepts EventSourceResponse as a response type.

name: _get_app
docstring: Import (or re-use) the FastAPI app with no real GCP calls.

name: _override_auth
docstring:
Override the auth dependency using the correct callable key.

FastAPI dependency_overrides must be keyed by the dependency callable
(verify_google_token), NOT by the Annotated type alias (CurrentUser).

name: _reload_observability
docstring: Reload the module so the module-level _tracer global is reset.

name: TestSetupTracing.test_silently_degrades_when_packages_missing
docstring: setup_tracing must not raise even if opentelemetry is not installed.

name: TestAgentSpan.test_span_context_manager_yields
docstring: agent_span must work as a context manager even with NoopTracer.

name: TestAgentSpan.test_span_uses_agent_name_in_span_name
docstring: The real tracer should be called with 'agent/<name>'.

name: patched_rag
docstring: Return a context manager that patches rag.retrieval_query per call.

name: _make_mock_module
docstring: Return a fully-faked vertexai.preview.memory_bank module.

name: test_register_fallback_when_vertex_unavailable
docstring: When Vertex AI import fails, registry should fall back gracefully.

name: TestGetConfiguredMcpTools.test_skips_toolset_when_factory_returns_none
docstring: If an import fails inside the factory, result is None and should be skipped.

name: TestScreenPrompt.test_disabled_when_template_id_blank
docstring: When MODEL_ARMOR_TEMPLATE_ID is empty, prompt is always allowed.

name: TestScreenPrompt.test_timeout_allows_through
docstring: A timeout must never block a legitimate user — allow-through.

name: _get_bq_client
docstring: Return a cached BigQuery client. Created once per project_id per process.

name: _run_query
docstring:
Execute a read-only BigQuery GoogleSQL query and return up to MAX_ROWS rows.

Args:
    project_id: GCP project ID.
    query: GoogleSQL SELECT statement.
    params: Optional dict of query parameters {name: value}.

Returns:
    dict with keys: rows (list[dict]), total_rows (int), schema (list[str]).
    On error, returns {"error": "<message>"}.

name: run_bigquery_query
docstring:
Module-level BigQuery query function for direct tool use (e.g. TaskAgent).

Args:
    query: A GoogleSQL SELECT statement.
    params: Optional dict mapping parameter names to values.

Returns:
    A dict with 'rows', 'total_rows', 'schema', or 'error' on failure.

name: _calendar_service
docstring: Return a cached Calendar API service for the given impersonated user.

name: create_calendar_event
signature: create_calendar_event(summary, start_datetime, end_datetime, attendees, description, location, timezone)
docstring:
Create a Google Calendar event and invite attendees.

Args:
    summary: Event title.
    start_datetime: Start time in ISO 8601 format: "2025-08-15T10:00:00".
    end_datetime: End time in ISO 8601 format:   "2025-08-15T11:00:00".
    attendees: Comma-separated list of attendee email addresses.
    description: Optional description / agenda for the event.
    location: Optional meeting location or video link.
    timezone: IANA timezone for the event. Defaults to "UTC".
        Examples: "America/New_York", "Asia/Bangkok", "Europe/London"

Returns:
    dict with keys: status, event_id, event_link, message

name: list_calendar_events
docstring:
List calendar events in a date range.

Args:
    start_date: Start of range in ISO 8601 date format: "2025-08-01".
    end_date: End of range in ISO 8601 date format: "2025-08-31".
    max_results: Maximum number of events to return (1-50). Defaults to 10.

Returns:
    dict with key "events": list of {id, summary, start, end, attendees, location}

name: check_availability
docstring:
Check whether a set of attendees are free during a time slot.

Args:
    attendees: Comma-separated list of attendee email addresses.
    start_datetime: Proposed start time: "2025-08-15T10:00:00".
    end_datetime: Proposed end time: "2025-08-15T11:00:00".
    timezone: IANA timezone for the window. Defaults to "UTC".

Returns:
    dict with key "availability": list of {email, busy_periods, is_free}

name: _drive_service
docstring: Return a cached Drive API service for the given impersonated user.

name: search_drive_files
docstring:
Search for files in Google Drive.

Args:
    query: Full-text or metadata query using Drive query syntax.
        Examples:
          "name contains 'Q3 report'"
          "mimeType='application/vnd.google-apps.document'"
          "fullText contains 'onboarding' and trashed=false"
          "'team-folder-id' in parents"
    max_results: Maximum number of results to return (1-50). Defaults to 10.

Returns:
    dict with key "files": list of {id, name, mime_type, modified_time, web_link}

name: read_drive_file
docstring:
Read the text content of a Google Drive file.

Supports Google Docs, Sheets (as CSV), Slides (as text), and plain-text files.
Returns the first 8,000 characters of content.

Args:
    file_id: The Drive file ID (from search_drive_files results).

Returns:
    dict with keys: id, name, mime_type, content (text), truncated (bool)

name: list_drive_folder
docstring:
List the files inside a Google Drive folder.

Args:
    folder_id: The Drive folder ID. Use "root" for My Drive.
    max_results: Maximum number of files to return (1-50). Defaults to 20.

Returns:
    dict with key "files": list of {id, name, mime_type, modified_time, web_link}

name: _gmail_service
docstring: Return a cached Gmail API service for the given impersonated user.

name: send_email
docstring:
Send an email on behalf of the configured Workspace admin user.

Args:
    to: Recipient email address (or comma-separated list).
    subject: Email subject line.
    body: Email body text.
    cc: Optional CC addresses (comma-separated).
    body_type: "plain" or "html". Defaults to "plain".

Returns:
    dict with keys: status, message_id, message

name: search_emails
docstring:
Search emails in the Gmail inbox.

Args:
    query: Gmail search query string (same syntax as Gmail search bar).
        Examples:
          "from:boss@company.com subject:report"
          "is:unread label:inbox newer_than:3d"
          "has:attachment filename:pdf"
    max_results: Maximum number of emails to return (1-50). Defaults to 10.

Returns:
    dict with key "emails": list of {id, from, to, subject, date, snippet}

name: get_email
docstring:
Read the full content of a specific email by its ID.

Args:
    message_id: The Gmail message ID (from search_emails results).

Returns:
    dict with keys: id, from, to, subject, date, body, attachments

name: make_filesystem_mcp_toolset
docstring:
Return an ADK MCPToolset backed by the official MCP filesystem server.

The server is launched as a subprocess via npx on first use.
Requires Node.js / npx installed on the host.

Args:
    path: Absolute directory the agent is allowed to read/write.

Returns:
    MCPToolset instance or None if ADK MCP support is unavailable.

name: make_sse_mcp_toolset
docstring:
Return an ADK MCPToolset connected to a remote SSE MCP server.

Args:
    server_url:  Full URL of the MCP SSE endpoint.
    auth_token:  Optional Bearer token sent in every request header.

Returns:
    MCPToolset instance or None if ADK MCP support is unavailable.

name: get_configured_mcp_tools
docstring:
Build and return all MCP toolsets that are configured in settings.

Each toolset is a single object that ADK expands into individual
FunctionTools at agent-build time, so pass the list directly to
LlmAgent(tools=[...]).

Returns an empty list when no MCP servers are configured.

name: _call_armor
docstring:
POST to a Model Armor sanitize operation.
Returns {} (allow-through) if the template is not configured, the
service is unreachable, or any transient error occurs.

name: screen_prompt
docstring:
Screen a user prompt before sending to the agent.
Catches prompt injections, jailbreaks, and PII in the user's message.

name: screen_response
docstring:
Screen a model response before returning it to the user.
Catches accidental PII leakage or toxic content in agent output.

name: schedule_agent_task
docstring:
Schedule this agent to run a task automatically at a future time or on a
recurring schedule.

Args:
    task_description: The task the agent should perform when triggered.
        Example: "Send a weekly sales report to the team."
    schedule: A cron expression OR a one-time RFC3339 datetime string.
        Cron examples:  "0 9 * * 1"  (every Monday at 9 AM)
                        "*/15 * * * *" (every 15 minutes)
                        "0 8 * * *"   (every day at 8 AM)
        One-time: use "cron" with a specific pattern for one-shot runs;
                  for truly one-off, create a job and delete it after it fires.
    job_name: A short lowercase identifier for this job.
        Example: "weekly-report" or "health-check-15m"
    timezone: IANA timezone for the schedule. Defaults to "UTC".
        Examples: "America/New_York", "Asia/Tokyo", "Europe/London"
    description: Optional human-readable description of why this job exists.

Returns:
    dict with keys:
        - job_name: full resource name of the created/updated job
        - schedule: the cron expression used
        - status: "created" | "updated" | "error"
        - message: human-readable result

name: delete_scheduled_task
docstring:
Delete a previously scheduled agent task.

Args:
    job_name: The short job name used when creating the schedule (e.g. "weekly-report").

Returns:
    dict with keys:
        - status: "deleted" | "not_found" | "error"
        - message: human-readable result

name: list_scheduled_tasks
search_knowledge_base
docstring:
Module-level knowledge base search for direct tool use (e.g. TaskAgent).

Args:
    query: Natural language search query.
    top_k: Maximum number of results to return.

Returns:
    List of dicts with 'text' and 'score' keys.

name: _get_gcs_client
docstring: Return a cached GCS client. Created once per process.

name: _safe_path
docstring: Return the path if safe, else None.

name: read_gcs_file
docstring:
Module-level GCS read for direct tool use (e.g. TaskAgent).

Args:
    bucket: GCS bucket name.
    path: Object path within the bucket.

Returns:
    Dict with 'content' or 'error'.

name: write_gcs_file
docstring:
Module-level GCS write for direct tool use (e.g. TaskAgent).

Args:
    bucket: GCS bucket name.
    path: Object path within the bucket.
    content: String content to write.

Returns:
    Dict with 'uri' or 'error'.

name: streamChat
signature:
streamChat({
  message,
  idToken,
  sessionId,
  onChunk,
  onDone,
  onError,
}: StreamChatOptions)
